visa_types.name: Au Pair
visa_types.overview: As an au pair, you can improve your language skills and learn about Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian family. In return, you will provide services such as light housework and/or child care for the host family.
visa_types.full_text:
As an au pair, you can improve your language skills and learn about Norwegian society by living with a Norwegian family. In return, you will provide services such as light housework and/or child care for the host family.

## Who can become an au pair?
- You must  pay an application fee. (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3592)
- You must be over the age of 18 and not have turned 30. 
- You cannot have any children of your own.
- It must be likely that you will return to your home country at the end of your stay in Norway, and the circumstances in your home country must also indicate that you can return.
- Please click below link for more information https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/au-pair/?c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/au-pair/?c=chl)

visa_types.name: Family Reunification
visa_types.overview:
You can apply for family immigration if you wish to move to a family member in Norway.
Eligible family members are:
- Spouses
- Cohabitants and fiancés/fianceés
- Children and parents
- Please click below link for more information https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/?f=chl&c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/?f=chl&c=chl)
visa_types.full_text:
You can apply for family immigration if you wish to move to a family member in Norway.
Eligible family members are:

- Please click below link for more information https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/?f=chl&c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/?f=chl&c=chl)

visa_types.name: Student
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to study or go to school in Norway for more than three months, you must apply for a study permit. If you are granted a study permit, you will also be able to work for up to 20 hours a week while you are studying and full-time during holidays.
Some possibilities are:
- University college or University
- Upper secondary school
- Folk high school and bible school
- Additional education in order for your education to be recognized in Norway
- Please click below link for more information https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/?c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/?c=chl)

visa_types.name: Other Permits
visa_types.overview:
There are several permits to apply to if you are going to visit or live in Norway, or are applying for citizenship or an immigrant's passport. Some of the options are:
- Please click below link for more information https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/?c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/?c=chl)

visa_types.name: Work
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
There are several categories to apply, some of them are:
- Skilled workers
- Seasonal workers
- Job seekers
- Exchange programs, culture and organizational work
- Vocational training and research
- Please click below link for more information
https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=chl)-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=chl (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=chl)
